paper_definition: Compelling users to believe that an application is harmless.
mapping_notes: Reported by authors as indirectly related to interpersonal conformity.

paper_definition: Ensuring that the application provides expected information and behavior.
mapping_notes: Authors report no clear corresponding Schwartz value.

paper_definition: Supporting a range of user devices and operating systems.
mapping_notes: Mapped due to relation with access to technical resources.

paper_definition: Allowing the application to be migrated to other devices.
mapping_notes: Reported as having a primary and secondary mapping.

paper_definition: Reducing the occurrence of application failures.
mapping_notes: Authors explicitly state difficulty mapping to Schwartz values.

paper_definition: Reducing the time and effort required to perform tasks within the application.
mapping_notes: Mapped due to efficient use of user resources.

paper_definition: Reducing power or energy consumption during application use.
Improving the ease of use of the application.
mapping_notes: Mapped as supporting users’ well-being and ease.

paper_definition: Improving access to information or functionality, particularly for users with special needs.
Ensuring prolonged availability of the application in the future.
mapping_notes: Related to users’ sense of future assurance.
